accusations.method: Petra poisoned Marcus with hydrogen cyanide dissolved in champagne. She delivered a 'special toast' glass to Marcus at 10:15 PM containing the poison. She then used a rubber tube inserted through the loosened ventilation grate to spray or introduce additional cyanide vapor into the Restoration Room between 10:30-10:50 PM (when kitchen staff were on break), ensuring a lethal dose. The bitter almond smell in the champagne and the cyanide traces in the glass confirm poisoning via the drink.
accusations.motive: Petra harbored deep resentment over her bitter breakup with Marcus 6 months ago. The announcement of the gallery sale—which would have eliminated her catering contract and any remaining connection to Marcus—pushed her to act. This was a crime of passion combined with the practical opportunity her catering role provided.
accusations.locked_room: The locked room was a red herring created by Marcus himself in his final moments. Marcus drank the poisoned champagne at 10:15 PM but didn't die immediately. Between 10:30-10:50 PM, as the poison took effect and he realized what was happening, he wrote the warning note 'Check the vents — she knows' while in distress. He locked the deadbolt from inside (the key was in his pocket) either to prevent discovery of his condition or in a final moment of clarity. The ventilation grate with loosened screws and the rubber tube were Petra's delivery mechanism, not an escape route. She never needed to enter the room.
